title: Before the Gates
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 15. Opens on the hard ride ahead of Arthas's main column and closes at Stratholme's gates when Jaina has to admit the source plan cannot save the city cleanly anymore.
openLocation: North road into Stratholme's outer farming belt.
transport: Hard horseback approach, then rapid on-foot work through storehouses and market routes, then return to the gates as the main army comes up.
immediateObjective: Reach Stratholme before the army, hit the plague's feeding points, and prove there is still a way to stop the city from turning.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
No interior access to Sylvanas, Arthas, or Uther.
No on-page Culling yet; the chapter stops at the gate answer rather than the purge itself.
No clean forgiveness between Jaina and Sylvanas; any repair must stay inside labor, timing, and placement.

povKnowsAtOpen: Hearthglen proved the outbreak can tip from bad grain and sick households into a city actively feeding itself to the dead.
Kel'Thuzad's explanation gave her something usable, not only explanatory: the plague can be broken at concentrated feeding points if she reaches them before too much of the city has eaten.
Arthas is marching behind them and will not wait for proof longer than the road makes him wait.
Sylvanas chose to ride with the source plan instead of staying with the prince's main force.
The crack from Scholomance is still there even though the team is working cleanly.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Stratholme is still early enough for the source plan to matter.
How many grain nodes in the city are already hot.
Whether any part of Arthas still wants an answer other than steel.
Whether she can tell Sylvanas 'too late' out loud if that is what the city says back.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No clean forgiveness between Jaina and Sylvanas.
No claim that the source plan was doomed from the start.
No interior access to Sylvanas or Arthas.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Jaina's source plan was real and works at the node level: at least one granary can be spoiled and its pull broken.
The city is already too far gone for that narrow success to scale. Too many stores are hot, too much bread is already in houses, and too much plague is now sitting inside living bodies.
Cult handlers are still present in the city and can harden or direct the dead when the choir alone would only clump and feed.
Sylvanas has direct field proof that Jaina used Kel'Thuzad's teaching to try to save people immediately.
Arthas reaches the gates and forces Jaina to answer the only question that matters: can Stratholme still be saved cleanly?

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If she can get to the right places fast enough, being right may finally save somebody instead of only explaining why they died.
pov.surfaceObjective: Break the plague's feeding points before the city finishes passing them through itself.
pov.pressureSource: The clock behind her, the city ahead, Arthas's speed, and Sylvanas riding close enough to make every choice visible.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina still wants correct theory plus speed to be enough.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can take a true field shot, watch it fail at scale, and still speak the truth without softening it.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep reaching for one more source node, or stop the attempt in time to tell Arthas the city cannot be saved the way she hoped.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina stops asking whether the source plan is elegant enough and starts counting how many mouths already outran it.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 17 opens with Sylvanas holding the gate line after Jaina's answer while Arthas decides what 'too late' means.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Rider, cover, witness, and the partner who makes this attempt field-real instead of theoretical.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina can feel that Sylvanas is watching not just whether the plan works, but what Jaina does with knowledge that once looked dangerous in her hands.
throughPov.interactionRule: Do not enter Sylvanas's interior. Use proximity, timing, questions, and where Sylvanas puts her body when Jaina has to work.

team.label: partnership
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The repair comes through labor. Jaina names targets, Sylvanas makes room for the work, and neither wastes breath on what cannot be solved on horseback.
team.namingAttitudeRule: The chapter must never call this proof or trust in the abstract. Let the proof live in the attempt itself.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No emotional summary language and no repair speech; the chapter only earns action-based re-alignment.

entries.rule: Tactical cover, hard questions, fast read on streets and lines of retreat.
Relay, horse control, flank watch, and second set of eyes on crowd shift and route closure.
Arrives late in the chapter, already moving. Keep him exterior and immediate.
Arrives with the main column. His presence must register as moral weight before the split.
Use them to show what 'too late' means in lived terms: bread already moved, ovens already hot, families already carrying the plague inside them.
Living field presence. Brief, practical, and alarming because she proves someone is still steering parts of this.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 17 opens at the gates after Jaina has answered Arthas honestly and before the city has been formally reduced to his next decision.

factsLockedForChapter17: Jaina's source plan was real and produced at least one local success.
That success cannot scale because too much contaminated grain has already moved into ovens, homes, and bodies.
Cult handlers are still active and can directly command or harden undead behavior inside the city.
Sylvanas has firsthand proof that Jaina used Kel'Thuzad's teaching to try to save people immediately.
Arthas has reached the gate and forced the question.
Jaina answered honestly that Stratholme cannot be saved cleanly now.
factsNotYetEarned: The on-page wording of Arthas's final order.
Any clean emotional reconciliation between Jaina and Sylvanas.

scenes.title: The Ride
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn the source plan into a physical race and make clear, through the way Jaina works, that this knowledge is being used to save lives rather than admired for its own sake.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The plan is narrow, the army is behind them, and the city clock is running.
scenes.arcPressure: If Jaina talks like a scholar here, she loses the chapter. The plan has to live in roads, districts, ovens, and arrival order.

enrichment.detail: Stratholme should already feel like a living northern city whose bread traffic moves through Market Row bakeries, warehouse yards, and service roads, so Jaina's ride-in logic reads as a race against urban distribution speed rather than a single warehouse job. Canon and local references both support Stratholme as a major holy and commercial city fed by Andorhal flour over multiple approach routes.

beats.goal: Reach Stratholme before Arthas's main force closes the distance.
beats.conflict: The road will not give them all the speed they want. Wagons have torn the ruts deep, the horses are already hot from the last stretch, and every slow bend in the road feels like bread cooling in another oven they have not reached yet.

outcome.summary: They keep the head start, but the road itself keeps reminding Jaina how small that lead is.

beats.valueShift: possible rescue -> rescue under a shrinking clock
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's mind is no longer on the whole plague chain; it is on delivery order and how fast bread leaves heat.
Sylvanas and Cyndia are treating the plan as a field task, not humoring it.
The main army's dust is still visible far enough back to matter.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Which district of Stratholme took the first seeded grain.
sensoryAnchors: horses blowing hard and white at the mouth
cart ruts forcing the firmer shoulder
main-column dust hanging low behind them
beats.transitionIntent: operational-briefing: once the ride is established, the plan has to be spoken in names a soldier can use, not only in mage terms.

beats.goal: Give Sylvanas the exact order of targets and what Jaina needs at each one.
beats.conflict: Jaina has to talk while riding and strip the plan down to what matters: main grain stores first, then bakehouses already working from them, then any district carts not yet unloaded. She can foul a store, break its resonance, and burn what must be burned. She cannot unfeed people who already ate.

outcome.summary: The team locks into a shared working picture, but the plan's weakness is named in the same breath as its strength.

beats.valueShift: rescue under a shrinking clock -> practical plan with a visible limit
newOnPageInformation: The source plan depends on concentration and order, not a city-wide wave of magic.
Jaina is openly using Kel'Thuzad's method against the plague, which Sylvanas hears in terms she can test.
Cyndia takes the route and relay role without argument, showing the trio is working as a unit.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the first store they hit will still be intact enough to matter.
Whether the city has already spread too far beyond stores and ovens.
sensoryAnchors: district names shouted into the wind
Sylvanas riding close enough to hear once
Cyndia cutting ahead at a fork
beats.transitionIntent: proof-demand: the next move has to prove the plan can do something real before the chapter asks it to fail.

scenes.title: The First Stores
scenes.sceneFunction: Give the source plan one real success, then show why that success cannot scale fast enough to save Stratholme.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The plan is physically workable if the target is concentrated and early enough.
scenes.arcPressure: One small win is necessary. One small win is also not enough.

enrichment.detail: Pre-Culling Stratholme should still feel prosperous and inhabited — market streets, family kitchens, service lanes, and multiple grain routes all active at once. Canon references to Market Row, civic districts, and the service entrance help ground why one spoiled granary can matter locally while failing at city scale.

beats.goal: Reach the nearest large grain store before the city moves too much more of it.
beats.conflict: The granary is still standing and still guarded, which gives Jaina one clean shot, but nobody at the door understands why a mage is screaming about spoiled grain while the city is still trying to open for trade.

outcome.summary: They force entry and secure the site, but the delay costs them the calm they needed.

beats.valueShift: practical plan with a visible limit -> first target still reachable
newOnPageInformation: Stratholme is not yet openly dead at the edges; parts of the city are still functioning on habit.
Local guards are confused rather than informed.
The outbreak is early enough here that a concentrated source might still be hit before it fully spreads.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How many other stores in the city are already moving the same grain.
Whether this granary is primary or only one branch.
sensoryAnchors: sack dust under high beams
teamsters arguing over deliveries
sickly sweet wrongness rising at the grain itself
beats.transitionIntent: node-test: the plan has to work here, visibly, or nothing later carries weight.

beats.goal: Break the plague's hold on the granary before more of it leaves the building.
beats.conflict: Jaina has only minutes and no proper circle room, only sacks, beams, flour dust, and a floor rough with spilled grain. Sylvanas and Cyndia hold the space while she forces the store to answer wrong: spoil, clot, collapse, go dead.

outcome.summary: The node breaks. The grain slumps uselessly, the pull in the room dies, and the first real success lands. But carts have already left.

beats.valueShift: first target still reachable -> proof the plan works in one place
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can foul a concentrated plague source in the field.
Sylvanas directly enables that work instead of doubting or slowing it.
The method is ugly, fast, and practical rather than elegant.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether they can repeat this enough times before the city outruns them.
How far the carts that already left have gone.
sensoryAnchors: grain souring in a rush
flour turning wet and gray
Jaina's hands shaking after the pull breaks
beats.transitionIntent: limit-exposure: the win has to show its limit immediately or it reads as false rescue.

beats.goal: Chase the next node before the first success turns into false hope.
beats.conflict: The street outside kills the illusion quickly. Bakers are already carrying morning loaves. Carts have already unloaded. Women are already tearing bread for children in doorways. Jaina cannot unbake what has already been eaten.

outcome.summary: The first win stands, but the city has already outrun it and turned that success into proof of how late they are.

beats.valueShift: proof the plan works in one place -> the city is already ahead of the plan
newOnPageInformation: Distribution speed, not only contamination, is now the problem.
The plague has already crossed from stores into kitchens and bodies.
Jaina now has to count people, not only nodes.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether a central store remains that matters enough to justify another push.
How many households are already carrying dormant activation.
sensoryAnchors: warm bread steam in an untrusted street
a mother breaking a loaf with flour on her wrist
ordinary market traffic reading like a delayed kill zone
beats.transitionIntent: last-test: the chapter now has to test whether there is still any point in striking one more node after seeing the bread already in hands.

beats.goal: Hit one more major store and find out whether the city can still be bent back at all.
beats.conflict: The second site is worse: already half stripped, already carrying sickness among the workers, and already watched by a living handler using taught command signals to stiffen the nearest dead when panic starts. The choir is real. So is deliberate direction.

outcome.summary: They break the second store, but the city answers by showing both spread and command. This is not one bad warehouse problem anymore.

beats.valueShift: the city is already ahead of the plan -> the city is both seeded and steered
newOnPageInformation: Cult handlers are still present and can directly shape undead compliance in the city.
The plague has enough blind self-organization to keep pressure up even without a handler on every street.
Jaina's method can destroy supply, but not erase what already moved into flesh.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any third strike is worth the time.
What answer Arthas will demand if he reaches the gates before they finish counting the damage.
sensoryAnchors: a human whistle pattern cutting through panic
sick workers standing too straight for too long
Sylvanas's arrow pinning the handler mid-turn
beats.transitionIntent: gate-return: the chapter now owes the cleanest and ugliest thing Jaina can say at city scale.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beats 3-6 continue the chapter's uninterrupted scene run. Keep the granary win, the bread-in-hands reversal, and the handler reveal sharply separated so the middle does not flatten into one long decline.

scenes.title: The Count
scenes.sceneFunction: Force Jaina to stop chasing one more fix and say the city-scale truth before Arthas turns uncertainty into policy.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The method works locally, but the city has already distributed too much plague into households and bodies.
scenes.arcPressure: If Jaina cannot say 'too late,' Arthas will fill the silence with his own answer anyway.

enrichment.detail: Stratholme is the birthplace of the Silver Hand, so Uther arriving at its gate carries moral and institutional weight before he says a word. Arthas's prince-paladin status and Jaina's prior relationship make the clean yes-or-no demand at the gate feel personal, military, and sacred all at once.

beats.goal: Decide whether the team should keep striking stores or break off for the gates.
beats.conflict: Jaina can still think of more places to hit. That is the problem. Each one might help one district, one morning, one street. None of them pulls the plague back out of stomachs, ovens, and blood. The plan is no longer failing because it is wrong. It is failing because it arrived after the city had already eaten.

outcome.summary: Jaina abandons the fantasy of one more useful stop, but doing so means she has to carry a worse answer back to the gates.

beats.valueShift: the city is both seeded and steered -> the truth is now human, not technical
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's break point is not magical exhaustion but the count itself.
She understands the exact way in which the plan lost: not bad theory, bad timing.
The line between source contamination and population contamination is now irretrievably crossed.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas will hear the difference between 'cannot be saved cleanly' and 'nothing can be done.'
Whether Uther will stand with Jaina's answer or against Arthas's response.
sensoryAnchors: open shutters tied to breakfasts already eaten
sour necromantic drag mixed with kitchen smoke
hoofbeats from the main column finally no longer imagined
beats.transitionIntent: command-arrival: the army reaches the gates, and Jaina no longer has the right to stay inside the problem instead of naming it.

beats.goal: Give Arthas the true answer before he makes the city answer for her silence.
beats.conflict: Arthas arrives already expecting the world to be narrowed into a usable yes or no. Jaina would like one more hour and knows an hour would change nothing. Uther is present. Sylvanas is present. The city is behind them. Arthas asks if Stratholme can still be saved.

outcome.summary: Jaina answers honestly: not cleanly, not now, not before the plague finishes what it has already started inside the living.

beats.valueShift: the truth is now human, not technical -> the city has no clean rescue left
newOnPageInformation: Jaina will not lie to buy herself comfort or buy Arthas false hope.
Arthas now has the one answer he was never going to hear as anything but a hard edge.
Uther hears the same words and understands exactly what they are about to cost.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The exact form Arthas's next order will take.
Where Sylvanas and Jaina will stand once that order is spoken.
sensoryAnchors: Arthas's horse still lathered from the ride up
gate guards flattening against stone
flour dust and ash in Jaina's mouth
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-bridge: Chapter 17 opens on Sylvanas holding this answer at the gate while Arthas turns it into action.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beat 7 has to make Jaina's stop-striking decision feel earned in bodies, ovens, and counts rather than abstract plague math alone, or beat 8's answer risks reading as exhaustion instead of choice.

warnings: Beats 1-8 are eight consecutive scene beats without a sequel. The chapter's breathless shape is intentional, but the draft will need sharper pressure differentiation so the ride, node strike, civilian spillover, handler reveal, and gate answer do not blur together.

Beats 2-4 rise in the same direction while beats 5-8 fall in the same direction. Keep the practical-plan lock-in, the local granary proof, the city-scale failure, and the gate truth categorically distinct so the pressure does not flatten into monotonic climb-and-collapse.

Outcome variety is narrow: beats 1-4 all land yes-but and beats 5-8 all land no-and, with no clean yes anywhere in the chapter. The beat-4 granary win has to read as a real local success or the later failure will feel like fake hope instead of timing failure.

The outline's meta info referenced stories/the-plague-road/locations/stratholme.md, but current project materials ground Stratholme through the Stratholme section in stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md. The normalized plan corrects the reference path while preserving the chapter's Stratholme research needs.
